SEARCH
Package io.github.simplejdbcmapper.core

Class SimpleJdbcMapper

java.lang.Object
io.github.simplejdbcmapper.core.SimpleJdbcMapper
public final class SimpleJdbcMapper extends Object
CRUD methods and configuration for SimpleJdbcMapper. 
 SimpleJdbcMapper should always be prepared in a Spring application context
 and given to services as a bean reference. It maintains state, for example
 caches insert/update SQL etc.
 
  Note: An instance of SimpleJdbcMapper is thread safe once configured.
Author:
Antony Joseph

  • Method Details

    • findById

      public <T> T findById(Class<T> entityType, Object id)
      finds the object by Id. Returns null if not found
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type
      Parameters:
      entityType - the type of object
      id - id of object
      Returns:
      the object of type T

    • findAll

      public <T> List<T> findAll(Class<T> entityType, SortBy... sortByArray)
      Find all objects.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type
      Parameters:
      entityType - type of object
      sortByArray - optional argument. An array of SortBy objects that are used to generate the "ORDER BY" clause
      Returns:
      List of objects of type T

    • findByPropertyValue

      public <T> List<T> findByPropertyValue(Class<T> entityType, String propertyName, Object propertyValue, SortBy... sortByArray)
      Returns list of objects which match the property value. 'IS NULL' clause will be used in the sql for a null value.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type
      Parameters:
      entityType - type of objects to be returned
      propertyName - the property name
      propertyValue - the property value
      sortByArray - optional argument. An array of SortBy objects that are used to generate the "ORDER BY" clause
      Returns:
      a List of objects of type T

    • findByPropertyValues

      public <T, U> List<T> findByPropertyValues(Class<T> entityType, String propertyName, Collection<U> propertyValues, SortBy... sortByArray)
      Returns list of objects which match the collection of property values. Uses an sql 'IN' clause. Large number of values could cause query performance degradation. Also different databases have different number/size limitations for sql 'IN" clauses. 
       Query is constructed in such a way that if there is a null value in the propertyValues
 the returned records will include records which match 'IS NULL' in the database.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type
      U - the type of the property values
      Parameters:
      entityType - the type of objects to be returned
      propertyName - the property name
      propertyValues - the collection of property values
      sortByArray - optional argument. An array of SortBy objects that are used to generate the "ORDER BY" clause
      Returns:
      a List of objects of type T

    • insert

      public void insert(Object object)
      Inserts an object. Objects with auto generated id will have the id set to the new id from database. For non auto generated id the id has to be manually set before invoking insert(). 
       Will handle the following annotations:
 @CreatedOn if Supplier is configured with SimpleJdbcMapper the property 
                will be assigned the supplied value
 @CreatedBy if Supplier is configured with SimpleJdbcMapper the property 
      will be assigned the supplied value
 @UpdatedOn if Supplier is configured with SimpleJdbcMapper the property 
                will be assigned the supplied value
 @UpdatedBy if Supplier is configured with SimpleJdbcMapper the property
                will be assigned the supplied value
 @Version property will be set to 1. Used for optimistic locking.
      Parameters:
      object - The object to be saved

    • update

      public Integer update(Object object)
      Update the object. 
       Will handle the following annotations:
 @UpdatedOn if Supplier is configured with SimpleJdbcMapper the property 
                will be assigned the supplied value
 @UpdatedBy if Supplier is configured with SimpleJdbcMapper the property 
                will be assigned the supplied value
 @Version property will be incremented on a successful update. An OptimisticLockingException
                will be thrown if object is stale.
      Parameters:
      object - object to be updated
      Returns:
      number of records updated

    • updateSpecificProperties

      public Integer updateSpecificProperties(Object object, String... propertyNames)
      Updates only the specified properties passed in as arguments. Use it to update a property or a few properties of the object and not the whole object. Issues an SQL update statement for only for the specific properties and any auto assign properties. 
       Will handle the following annotations:
 @UpdatedOn if Supplier is configured with SimpleJdbcMapper the property 
                will be assigned the supplied value
 @UpdatedBy if Supplier is configured with SimpleJdbcMapper the property 
                will be assigned the supplied value
 @Version property will be incremented on a successful update. An OptimisticLockingException
                will be thrown if object is stale.
      Parameters:
      object - object to be updated
      propertyNames - the specific property names that need to be updated.
      Returns:
      number of records updated

    • delete

      public Integer delete(Object object)
      Deletes the object from the database.
      Parameters:
      object - Object to be deleted
      Returns:
      number of records were deleted (1 or 0)

    • deleteById

      public Integer deleteById(Class<?> entityType, Object id)
      Deletes the object from the database by id.
      Parameters:
      entityType - type of object to be deleted.
      id - id of object to be deleted
      Returns:
      number records were deleted (1 or 0)

    • getBeanFriendlySqlColumns

      public String getBeanFriendlySqlColumns(Class<?> entityType)
      Gets the columns SQL. Works well with Spring row mappers like BeanPropertyRowMapper(), SimplePropertyRowMapper() etc. Will create the needed column aliases where the column name does not match the corresponding underscore case property name.

      It is a good practice to store this string, since every time this method is invoked the columns have to be concatenated along with some string manipulation

      Will return something like below if 'userLastName' property is mapped to 'last_name' column in database: 

       "somecolumn, someothercolumn, last_name AS user_last_name"
      Parameters:
      entityType - the type
      Returns:
      comma separated select column string

    • getBeanFriendlySqlColumns

      public String getBeanFriendlySqlColumns(Class<?> entityType, String tableAlias)
      Gets the columns SQL with columns prefixed with the table alias. Works well with Spring row mappers like BeanPropertyRowMapper(), SimplePropertyRowMapper() etc. Will prefix table column names with the 'tableAlias.' and will create the needed column aliases where the column name does not match the corresponding underscore case property name.

      Use it in your custom queries when you are doing joins and need columns corresponding to a table alias.

      It is a good practice to store this string, since every time this method is invoked the columns have to be concatenated long with some string manipulation.

      For tableAlias argument 't1' will return something like below if 'userLastName' property is mapped to 'last_name' column in database: 

       "t1.somecolumn, t1.someothercolumn, t1.last_name AS user_last_name"
      Parameters:
      entityType - the type
      tableAlias - the table alias
      Returns:
      comma separated select column string

    • getPropertyToColumnMappings

      public Map<String,String> getPropertyToColumnMappings(Class<?> entityType)
      returns a map with all the properties of the mapped class and their corresponding column names
      Parameters:
      entityType - the class
      Returns:
      map of property and their corresponding columns

      public org.springframework.jdbc.core.simple.JdbcClient getJdbcClient()

      Returns:

      public <T> void setRecordAuditedBySupplier(Supplier<T> supplier)
      Set the Supplier that is used to populate the @CreatedBy and @UpdatedBy annotated properties.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type
      Parameters:
      supplier - the Supplier for audited by.

    • setRecordAuditedOnSupplier

      public <T> void setRecordAuditedOnSupplier(Supplier<T> supplier)
      Set the Supplier that is used to populate the @CreatedOn and @UpdatedOn annotated properties.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type
      Parameters:
      supplier - the Supplier for audited on.

    • getConversionService

      public org.springframework.core.convert.ConversionService getConversionService()
      Exposing the conversion service used, so if necessary new converters can be added etc. The default conversion service used in SimpleJdbcMapper is Spring's DefaultConversionService.
      Returns:
      the conversion service.

    • setConversionService

      public void setConversionService(org.springframework.core.convert.ConversionService conversionService)
      Set the conversion service
      Parameters:
      conversionService - The conversion service to set

    • getSchemaName

      public String getSchemaName()
      Get the schema name.
      Returns:
      the schema name.

    • getCatalogName

      public String getCatalogName()
      Get the catalog name.
      Returns:
      the catalog name.
